---
title: "Vertiefung: Materialalterung"
author: "Stefan Jordan"
format:
  html:
    theme: cosmo
    toc: true
jupyter: python3
bibliography: ../references.bib
---

## Warum Materialalterung eine PBR-Frage ist

Auf den vorherigen Seiten wurden PBR-Materialien vor allem als Kombination aus
Parametern beschrieben: `Base Color`, `Metallic`, `Roughness` und die
Beleuchtung durch IBL. In einer echten Szene sind diese Werte jedoch selten
konstant. Ein Objekt altert, wird benutzt, oxidiert, verschmutzt oder wird an
Kanten wieder blank poliert.

Damit ist Alterung kein reiner "Farbfilter". Wenn Kupfer eine Patina bildet
oder Eisen rostet, verändert sich nicht nur die sichtbare Farbe der Oberfläche.
Auch das Reflexionsverhalten ändert sich:

- Die Oberfläche wird meist rauer.
- Die Spiegelung wird breiter und dunkler.
- Oxidierte oder verschmutzte Bereiche verhalten sich nicht mehr wie blankes Metall.
- Einzelne Regionen können sehr unterschiedliche Materialzustände besitzen.

Die zentrale Idee dieser Vertiefung lautet deshalb:

> **Realistische Alterung entsteht, wenn mehrere PBR-Maps gemeinsam verändert werden.**

## Interaktive Demo: Ein Kupferschild altert

Die folgende Demo verwendet bewusst noch kein externes 3D-Modell. Das Objekt
wird aus einfachen Babylon.js-Grundformen aufgebaut: eine leicht gewölbte
Schildfläche mit erhabenem Rand und Nieten. Der interessante Teil liegt aber
weiterhin in den prozedural erzeugten Texturen. Der Alterungsslider verändert
gleichzeitig eine Albedo-Map, eine Roughness-Map und eine Metallic-Map.

Die drei Modi zeigen, warum eine reine Farbänderung nicht ausreicht:

- **Nur Albedo:** Die Patina wird nur eingefärbt. Das Material bleibt trotzdem
  überall spiegelndes Metall.
- **Albedo + Roughness:** Gealterte Bereiche werden matter, bleiben aber noch
  metallisch.
- **Alle PBR-Maps:** Patina-Bereiche verlieren zusätzlich ihre metallische
  Reflexion. Erst dadurch wirkt die Oberfläche plausibel gealtert.

```{=html}
<style>
  #agingDemoGrid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 2fr) minmax(260px, 1fr);
    gap: 16px;
    align-items: start;
  }

  @media (max-width: 800px) {
    #agingDemoGrid {
      grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    }

    #agingCanvas {
      height: 380px !important;
    }
  }
</style>
<div style="background:#f8f9fa; padding:16px; border-radius:8px; margin:20px 0;">
  <div id="agingDemoGrid">
    <div style="min-width:0;">
      <canvas id="agingCanvas" style="width:100%; height:520px; border-radius:6px; outline:none; touch-action:none; background:#111;"></canvas>
    </div>

    <div style="display:flex; flex-direction:column; gap:14px;">
      <div>
        <label for="agingEnvSwitch" style="display:block; font-weight:600; margin-bottom:6px;">Umgebung</label>
        <select id="agingEnvSwitch" style="width:100%; padding:7px; border-radius:4px; border:1px solid #ccc;">
          <option value="https://playground.babylonjs.com/textures/environment.env">Stadt (Neutral)</option>
          <option value="https://playground.babylonjs.com/textures/country.env">Landlandschaft (Offen)</option>
          <option value="https://playground.babylonjs.com/textures/forest.env">Wald (Grünlich)</option>
          <option value="https://playground.babylonjs.com/textures/room.env">Innenraum (Warm)</option>
        </select>
      </div>

      <div>
        <label for="agingSlider" style="display:flex; justify-content:space-between; gap:12px; font-weight:600;">
          <span>Alterung</span>
          <span id="agingValue">0%</span>
        </label>
        <input id="agingSlider" type="range" min="0" max="100" step="1" value="0" style="width:100%;">
      </div>

      <div>
        <div style="font-weight:600; margin-bottom:6px;">Aktive Materialinformationen</div>
        <div style="display:flex; flex-wrap:wrap; gap:8px;">
          <button class="agingModeBtn" data-mode="color" style="padding:6px 10px; border:1px solid #888; border-radius:4px; background:#fff; cursor:pointer;">Nur Albedo</button>
          <button class="agingModeBtn" data-mode="rough" style="padding:6px 10px; border:1px solid #888; border-radius:4px; background:#fff; cursor:pointer;">Albedo + Roughness</button>
          <button class="agingModeBtn" data-mode="full" style="padding:6px 10px; border:1px solid #888; border-radius:4px; background:#212529; color:#fff; cursor:pointer;">Alle PBR-Maps</button>
        </div>
      </div>

      <div style="font-size:0.92em; color:#555;">
        <strong id="agingModeTitle">Alle PBR-Maps:</strong>
        <span id="agingModeText">Patina verändert Farbe, Rauheit und Metallizität gleichzeitig.</span>
      </div>

      <div style="display:grid; grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr 1fr; gap:8px;">
        <div>
          <div style="font-size:0.82em; font-weight:600; margin-bottom:4px;">Albedo</div>
          <canvas id="albedoPreview" width="256" height="256" style="width:100%; aspect-ratio:1; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:4px; image-rendering:auto;"></canvas>
        </div>
        <div>
          <div style="font-size:0.82em; font-weight:600; margin-bottom:4px;">Metallic</div>
          <canvas id="metallicPreview" width="256" height="256" style="width:100%; aspect-ratio:1; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:4px; image-rendering:auto;"></canvas>
        </div>
        <div>
          <div style="font-size:0.82em; font-weight:600; margin-bottom:4px;">Roughness</div>
          <canvas id="roughnessPreview" width="256" height="256" style="width:100%; aspect-ratio:1; border:1px solid #ccc; border-radius:4px; image-rendering:auto;"></canvas>
        </div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>
</div>

<script src="https://cdn.babylonjs.com/babylon.js"></script>
<script>
(function () {
  function initAgingDemo() {
    const canvas = document.getElementById("agingCanvas");
    if (!canvas || typeof BABYLON === "undefined") return;

    const slider = document.getElementById("agingSlider");
    const agingValue = document.getElementById("agingValue");
    const envSwitch = document.getElementById("agingEnvSwitch");
    const modeTitle = document.getElementById("agingModeTitle");
    const modeText = document.getElementById("agingModeText");
    const modeButtons = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(".agingModeBtn"));

    const engine = new BABYLON.Engine(canvas, true, { preserveDrawingBuffer: true, stencil: true });
    const scene = new BABYLON.Scene(engine);
    scene.clearColor = new BABYLON.Color4(0.11, 0.115, 0.12, 1);

    const camera = new BABYLON.ArcRotateCamera(
      "agingCamera",
      -Math.PI / 2,
      Math.PI / 2.22,
      3.5,
      new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 0, 0),
      scene
    );
    camera.attachControl(canvas, true);
    camera.lowerRadiusLimit = 2.6;
    camera.upperRadiusLimit = 5.2;
    camera.wheelPrecision = 60;

    scene.environmentIntensity = 0.95;
    let currentSkybox = null;

    function loadEnvironment(url) {
      const envTexture = BABYLON.CubeTexture.CreateFromPrefilteredData(url, scene);
      scene.environmentTexture = envTexture;
      if (currentSkybox) {
        currentSkybox.dispose();
      }
      currentSkybox = scene.createDefaultSkybox(envTexture, true, 80, 0.25);
    }

    loadEnvironment(envSwitch.value);
    envSwitch.addEventListener("change", (event) => {
      loadEnvironment(event.target.value);
    });

    const keyLight = new BABYLON.DirectionalLight("agingKeyLight", new BABYLON.Vector3(-0.6, -1.0, 0.4), scene);
    keyLight.intensity = 0.55;
    const fillLight = new BABYLON.HemisphericLight("agingFillLight", new BABYLON.Vector3(0, 1, 0), scene);
    fillLight.intensity = 0.18;

    const material = new BABYLON.PBRMaterial("agedCopperMaterial", scene);
    material.albedoColor = new BABYLON.Color3(1, 1, 1);
    material.metallic = 1.0;
    material.roughness = 1.0;
    material.environmentIntensity = 1.05;
    material.useMetallnessFromMetallicTextureBlue = true;
    material.useRoughnessFromMetallicTextureGreen = true;
    material.useRoughnessFromMetallicTextureAlpha = false;

    const root = new BABYLON.TransformNode("copperAssembly", scene);
    root.rotation.x = -0.04;

    function useAgedMaterial(mesh) {
      mesh.parent = root;
      mesh.material = material;
      return mesh;
    }

    function shieldHalfWidth(t) {
      if (t < 0.2) {
        return mix(0.92, 1.04, smoothstep(0.0, 0.2, t));
      }
      if (t < 0.68) {
        return mix(1.04, 0.74, smoothstep(0.2, 0.68, t));
      }
      return mix(0.74, 0.05, smoothstep(0.68, 1.0, t));
    }

    function shieldPoint(nx, t, zLift) {
      const halfWidth = shieldHalfWidth(t);
      const topDip = (1 - smoothstep(0.02, 0.16, t)) * -0.1 * (1 - nx * nx);
      const y = 1.08 - 2.34 * t + topDip;
      const x = nx * halfWidth;
      const centerBulge = Math.pow(1 - Math.min(1, Math.abs(nx)), 0.85) *
        Math.pow(Math.sin(Math.PI * clamp01(t)), 0.72);
      const z = -0.045 - centerBulge * 0.2 + zLift;
      return new BABYLON.Vector3(x, y, z);
    }

    const shieldRows = 44;
    const shieldCols = 38;
    const shieldPaths = [];
    for (let r = 0; r < shieldRows; r++) {
      const t = r / (shieldRows - 1);
      const row = [];
      for (let c = 0; c < shieldCols; c++) {
        const nx = c / (shieldCols - 1) * 2 - 1;
        row.push(shieldPoint(nx, t, 0));
      }
      shieldPaths.push(row);
    }

    useAgedMaterial(BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateRibbon("shieldBody", {
      pathArray: shieldPaths,
      closeArray: false,
      closePath: false,
      sideOrientation: BABYLON.Mesh.DOUBLESIDE
    }, scene));

    function outlinePath(scale, zLift) {
      const path = [];
      const sideSteps = 34;
      const topSteps = 28;

      for (let i = 0; i <= topSteps; i++) {
        const nx = -scale + (2 * scale * i) / topSteps;
        path.push(shieldPoint(nx, 0, zLift));
      }
      for (let i = 1; i <= sideSteps; i++) {
        const t = i / sideSteps;
        path.push(shieldPoint(scale, t, zLift));
      }
      for (let i = sideSteps - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
        const t = i / sideSteps;
        path.push(shieldPoint(-scale, t, zLift));
      }
      path.push(path[0].clone());
      return path;
    }

    useAgedMaterial(BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateTube("outerShieldRim", {
      path: outlinePath(0.98, -0.035),
      radius: 0.038,
      tessellation: 18,
      cap: BABYLON.Mesh.CAP_ALL
    }, scene));

    useAgedMaterial(BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateTube("innerShieldRim", {
      path: outlinePath(0.76, -0.055),
      radius: 0.024,
      tessellation: 16,
      cap: BABYLON.Mesh.CAP_ALL
    }, scene));

    const topBandPath = [];
    for (let i = 0; i <= 34; i++) {
      const x = -0.72 + (1.44 * i) / 34;
      const y = 0.66 - 0.05 * Math.cos((i / 34) * Math.PI * 2);
      const t = clamp01((1.08 - y) / 2.34);
      const nx = x / shieldHalfWidth(t);
      topBandPath.push(shieldPoint(nx, t, -0.012));
    }
    useAgedMaterial(BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateTube("raisedTopBand", {
      path: topBandPath,
      radius: 0.014,
      tessellation: 16,
      cap: BABYLON.Mesh.CAP_ALL
    }, scene));

    const ridgePath = [];
    for (let i = 0; i <= 32; i++) {
      const t = 0.18 + (0.72 * i) / 32;
      ridgePath.push(shieldPoint(0, t, -0.01));
    }
    useAgedMaterial(BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateTube("centerRidge", {
      path: ridgePath,
      radius: 0.01,
      tessellation: 16,
      cap: BABYLON.Mesh.CAP_ALL
    }, scene));

    const rivets = [
      [-0.64, 0.77], [-0.22, 0.81], [0.22, 0.81], [0.64, 0.77],
      [-0.86, 0.23], [0.86, 0.23],
      [-0.56, -0.7], [0.56, -0.7]
    ];

    rivets.forEach((position, index) => {
      const t = clamp01((1.08 - position[1]) / 2.34);
      const nx = position[0] / shieldHalfWidth(t);
      const point = shieldPoint(nx, t, -0.025);
      const rivet = useAgedMaterial(BABYLON.MeshBuilder.CreateSphere("rivet" + index, {
        diameter: 0.13,
        segments: 24
      }, scene));
      rivet.position.copyFrom(point);
      rivet.scaling.z = 0.42;
    });

    const texSize = 256;
    const albedoTex = new BABYLON.DynamicTexture("agingAlbedoTex", texSize, scene, false);
    const mrFullTex = new BABYLON.DynamicTexture("agingMRFullTex", texSize, scene, false);
    const mrRoughTex = new BABYLON.DynamicTexture("agingMRRoughTex", texSize, scene, false);
    [albedoTex, mrFullTex, mrRoughTex].forEach((tex) => {
      tex.wrapU = BABYLON.Texture.CLAMP_ADDRESSMODE;
      tex.wrapV = BABYLON.Texture.CLAMP_ADDRESSMODE;
    });
    material.albedoTexture = albedoTex;
    material.metallicTexture = mrFullTex;

    const previewAlbedo = document.getElementById("albedoPreview").getContext("2d");
    const previewMetallic = document.getElementById("metallicPreview").getContext("2d");
    const previewRoughness = document.getElementById("roughnessPreview").getContext("2d");

    let currentMode = "full";

    function clamp01(value) {
      return Math.max(0, Math.min(1, value));
    }

    function smoothstep(edge0, edge1, value) {
      const x = clamp01((value - edge0) / (edge1 - edge0));
      return x * x * (3 - 2 * x);
    }

    function mix(a, b, t) {
      return a * (1 - t) + b * t;
    }

    function fade(t) {
      return t * t * (3 - 2 * t);
    }

    function hash2(ix, iy) {
      const value = Math.sin(ix * 127.1 + iy * 311.7) * 43758.5453123;
      return value - Math.floor(value);
    }

    function valueNoise(u, v, scale) {
      const x = u * scale;
      const y = v * scale;
      const x0 = Math.floor(x);
      const y0 = Math.floor(y);
      const tx = fade(x - x0);
      const ty = fade(y - y0);
      const a = hash2(x0, y0);
      const b = hash2(x0 + 1, y0);
      const c = hash2(x0, y0 + 1);
      const d = hash2(x0 + 1, y0 + 1);
      return mix(mix(a, b, tx), mix(c, d, tx), ty);
    }

    function fbm(u, v) {
      return (
        valueNoise(u, v, 2.2) * 0.44 +
        valueNoise(u + 19.1, v - 7.4, 5.0) * 0.28 +
        valueNoise(u - 3.7, v + 11.8, 11.0) * 0.18 +
        valueNoise(u + 5.2, v + 2.6, 24.0) * 0.10
      );
    }

    function drawImageDataToTexture(texture, imageData) {
      const ctx = texture.getContext();
      ctx.putImageData(imageData, 0, 0);
      texture.update(false);
    }

    function drawPreview(ctx, sourceImageData, channel) {
      const preview = ctx.createImageData(texSize, texSize);
      for (let i = 0; i < sourceImageData.data.length; i += 4) {
        let value;
        if (channel === "metallic") {
          value = sourceImageData.data[i + 2];
        } else if (channel === "roughness") {
          value = sourceImageData.data[i + 1];
        } else {
          preview.data[i] = sourceImageData.data[i];
          preview.data[i + 1] = sourceImageData.data[i + 1];
          preview.data[i + 2] = sourceImageData.data[i + 2];
          preview.data[i + 3] = 255;
          continue;
        }
        preview.data[i] = value;
        preview.data[i + 1] = value;
        preview.data[i + 2] = value;
        preview.data[i + 3] = 255;
      }
      ctx.putImageData(preview, 0, 0);
    }

    const rivetUv = rivets.map((position) => {
      const t = clamp01((1.08 - position[1]) / 2.34);
      const u = clamp01(position[0] / (2 * shieldHalfWidth(t)) + 0.5);
      return [u, t];
    });

    const scratchSegments = [
      [0.28, 0.29, 0.39, 0.31, 0.0016],
      [0.58, 0.34, 0.68, 0.31, 0.0014],
      [0.34, 0.45, 0.47, 0.50, 0.0015],
      [0.63, 0.54, 0.74, 0.57, 0.0014],
      [0.25, 0.64, 0.35, 0.61, 0.0014],
      [0.43, 0.72, 0.53, 0.76, 0.0015],
      [0.67, 0.74, 0.76, 0.69, 0.0014],
      [0.36, 0.84, 0.44, 0.80, 0.0016],
      [0.52, 0.42, 0.58, 0.39, 0.0012],
      [0.46, 0.58, 0.53, 0.61, 0.0012],
      [0.59, 0.83, 0.66, 0.79, 0.0013]
    ];

    function distanceToSegment(px, py, x1, y1, x2, y2) {
      const vx = x2 - x1;
      const vy = y2 - y1;
      const wx = px - x1;
      const wy = py - y1;
      const lengthSquared = vx * vx + vy * vy;
      const t = lengthSquared === 0 ? 0 : clamp01((wx * vx + wy * vy) / lengthSquared);
      const dx = px - (x1 + vx * t);
      const dy = py - (y1 + vy * t);
      return Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
    }

    function scratchMaskAt(u, v) {
      let mask = 0;
      scratchSegments.forEach((scratch) => {
        const distance = distanceToSegment(u, v, scratch[0], scratch[1], scratch[2], scratch[3], scratch[4]);
        mask = Math.max(mask, 1 - smoothstep(scratch[4], scratch[4] * 2.8, distance));
      });
      return mask;
    }

    function rivetHaloAt(u, v) {
      let halo = 0;
      rivetUv.forEach((point) => {
        const dx = (u - point[0]) * 1.15;
        const dy = v - point[1];
        const distance = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);
        halo = Math.max(halo, smoothstep(0.115, 0.02, distance));
      });
      return halo;
    }

    function updateMaps() {
      const age = Number(slider.value) / 100;
      agingValue.textContent = Math.round(age * 100) + "%";

      const albedoData = new ImageData(texSize, texSize);
      const mrFullData = new ImageData(texSize, texSize);
      const mrRoughData = new ImageData(texSize, texSize);

      for (let y = 0; y < texSize; y++) {
        const v = y / (texSize - 1);
        for (let x = 0; x < texSize; x++) {
          const u = x / (texSize - 1);
          const index = (y * texSize + x) * 4;

          const nx = (u - 0.5) * 2;
          const sideEdge = smoothstep(0.72, 1.0, Math.abs(nx));
          const topEdge = 1 - smoothstep(0.03, 0.12, v);
          const lowerPoint = smoothstep(0.78, 1.0, v);
          const polishedEdge = clamp01(Math.max(sideEdge, topEdge * 0.6, lowerPoint * 0.75));
          const lowerArea = smoothstep(0.42, 1.0, v);
          const rivetHalo = rivetHaloAt(u, v);
          const scratch = scratchMaskAt(u, v);
          const largeNoise = fbm(u, v);
          const mediumNoise = fbm(u * 1.9 + 0.13, v * 1.55 - 0.21);
          const fineNoise = valueNoise(u + 3.1, v - 1.7, 58.0);
          const corrosionPotential = clamp01(
            0.48 * largeNoise +
            0.22 * mediumNoise +
            0.11 * lowerArea +
            0.16 * rivetHalo -
            0.22 * polishedEdge
          );
          const patina = smoothstep(0.7 - age * 0.31, 0.9 - age * 0.21, corrosionPotential) *
            smoothstep(0.18, 0.95, age) * (1 - polishedEdge * 0.55);
          const tarnish = clamp01(age * (0.22 + 0.28 * largeNoise));
          const rubbedEdge = polishedEdge * smoothstep(0.28, 0.95, age) * (1 - patina * 0.8);

          const copper = [184, 84, 37];
          const darkCopper = [92, 47, 30];
          const patinaColor = [
            mix(49, 86, mediumNoise),
            mix(114, 158, mediumNoise),
            mix(103, 136, mediumNoise)
          ];
          const polishedCopper = [225, 132, 68];

          let r = mix(copper[0], darkCopper[0], tarnish);
          let g = mix(copper[1], darkCopper[1], tarnish);
          let b = mix(copper[2], darkCopper[2], tarnish);

          r += (mediumNoise - 0.5) * 10;
          g += (mediumNoise - 0.5) * 7;
          b += (mediumNoise - 0.5) * 5;

          r = mix(r, patinaColor[0], patina);
          g = mix(g, patinaColor[1], patina);
          b = mix(b, patinaColor[2], patina);

          r = mix(r, polishedCopper[0], rubbedEdge * 0.55);
          g = mix(g, polishedCopper[1], rubbedEdge * 0.55);
          b = mix(b, polishedCopper[2], rubbedEdge * 0.55);

          const scratchStrength = scratch * (0.2 + age * 0.25) * (1 - patina * 0.7);
          r = mix(r, 58, scratchStrength);
          g = mix(g, 31, scratchStrength);
          b = mix(b, 20, scratchStrength);

          r = Math.round(clamp01(r / 255) * 255);
          g = Math.round(clamp01(g / 255) * 255);
          b = Math.round(clamp01(b / 255) * 255);

          const roughness = clamp01(0.22 + age * 0.18 + largeNoise * 0.08 + fineNoise * 0.025 + patina * 0.42 + scratchStrength * 0.12 - rubbedEdge * 0.16);
          const metallic = clamp01(1.0 - patina * 0.82 - tarnish * 0.045 + rubbedEdge * 0.06);

          albedoData.data[index] = r;
          albedoData.data[index + 1] = g;
          albedoData.data[index + 2] = b;
          albedoData.data[index + 3] = 255;

          mrFullData.data[index] = 0;
          mrFullData.data[index + 1] = Math.round(roughness * 255);
          mrFullData.data[index + 2] = Math.round(metallic * 255);
          mrFullData.data[index + 3] = 255;

          mrRoughData.data[index] = 0;
          mrRoughData.data[index + 1] = Math.round(roughness * 255);
          mrRoughData.data[index + 2] = 255;
          mrRoughData.data[index + 3] = 255;
        }
      }

      drawImageDataToTexture(albedoTex, albedoData);
      drawImageDataToTexture(mrFullTex, mrFullData);
      drawImageDataToTexture(mrRoughTex, mrRoughData);
      drawPreview(previewAlbedo, albedoData, "albedo");
      drawPreview(previewMetallic, mrFullData, "metallic");
      drawPreview(previewRoughness, mrFullData, "roughness");
    }

    function applyMode(mode) {
      currentMode = mode;
      modeButtons.forEach((button) => {
        const active = button.dataset.mode === mode;
        button.style.background = active ? "#212529" : "#fff";
        button.style.color = active ? "#fff" : "#212529";
      });

      if (mode === "color") {
        material.metallicTexture = null;
        material.metallic = 1.0;
        material.roughness = 0.22;
        modeTitle.textContent = "Nur Albedo:";
        modeText.textContent = "Die Patina wird sichtbar, reflektiert aber noch wie blankes Metall.";
      } else if (mode === "rough") {
        material.metallicTexture = mrRoughTex;
        material.metallic = 1.0;
        material.roughness = 1.0;
        modeTitle.textContent = "Albedo + Roughness:";
        modeText.textContent = "Gealterte Bereiche werden matter, bleiben aber physikalisch noch Metall.";
      } else {
        material.metallicTexture = mrFullTex;
        material.metallic = 1.0;
        material.roughness = 1.0;
        modeTitle.textContent = "Alle PBR-Maps:";
        modeText.textContent = "Patina verändert Farbe, Rauheit und Metallizität gleichzeitig.";
      }
    }

    slider.addEventListener("input", updateMaps);
    modeButtons.forEach((button) => {
      button.addEventListener("click", () => applyMode(button.dataset.mode));
    });

    updateMaps();
    applyMode(currentMode);

    engine.runRenderLoop(function () {
      root.rotation.y = Math.sin(performance.now() * 0.00028) * 0.09;
      scene.render();
    });

    window.addEventListener("resize", function () {
      engine.resize();
    });
  }

  if (document.readyState === "loading") {
    document.addEventListener("DOMContentLoaded", initAgingDemo);
  } else {
    initAgingDemo();
  }
})();
</script>
```

## Was an der Demo vereinfacht ist

Die Demo ist kein chemisch exaktes Korrosionsmodell. Sie simuliert nicht, wie
schnell Kupfer unter bestimmten Umweltbedingungen oxidiert. Für die PBR-Erklärung
ist das aber auch nicht der entscheidende Punkt. Wichtig ist die Kopplung der
Materialparameter:

- In der **Albedo-Map** entstehen dunkle und grünliche Bereiche.
- In der **Roughness-Map** werden diese Bereiche matter.
- In der **Metallic-Map** verlieren oxidierte Bereiche ihre metallische
  Reflexion.

Damit wird aus einem globalen Materialwert ein ortsabhängiger Materialzustand.
Das ist genau der Schritt, der in realen PBR-Workflows über Texture Maps,
Substance Painter, Blender oder prozedurale Shader umgesetzt wird.

## Ausblick: Vom prozeduralen Entwurf zum eigenen Asset

Dieser erste Entwurf lässt sich später erweitern, ohne das didaktische Konzept zu
ändern. Statt des einfachen prozeduralen Kupferschilds könnte ein eigenes CAD- oder
Blender-Modell verwendet werden, zum Beispiel ein Schild mit sauber modellierten
Falzen, Nieten, Kratzern und unterschiedlich stark gealterten Vertiefungen.

Dann würden die Alterungseffekte nicht nur aus einem 2D-Muster entstehen, sondern
gezielt an sinnvollen Stellen auftreten:

- stärkere Oxidation in Vertiefungen,
- polierte Kanten an häufig berührten Stellen,
- Schmutzansammlung an Fugen,
- unterschiedliche Alterung für Metall, Kunststoff oder Lack.

Der Kern bleibt aber gleich: Alterung ist in PBR keine einzelne Zahl, sondern eine
gemeinsame Veränderung mehrerer Maps.
